name: Korean Fried Chicken (Dakgangjeong)
why: You've been exploring Korean cuisine, and this is the crown jewel — double-fried wings in a sticky gochujang glaze. The cornflour coating technique gives an insane crunch that stays crispy even after saucing. Perfect summer comfort food.
author: Henry
ingredients: Large chunk of ginger, peeled and finely grated
Small piece of ginger, peeled and grated
Sesame seeds and sliced spring onion, to serve

steps: Put the sugar, gochujang, soy sauce, crushed garlic, grated ginger, and sesame oil in a saucepan.
Simmer over medium heat until syrupy and glossy, about 3-4 minutes. The sugar should fully dissolve.
Remove from heat and set aside.
Season the chicken wings with salt, pepper, and the grated ginger.
Toss the wings with cornflour until completely coated — every nook and cranny should be covered.
Shake off any excess cornflour that clumps together.
Heat about 2cm of vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat (around 170-180°C if you have a thermometer).
Fry the chicken wings for 8-10 minutes until golden and crisp, turning halfway through.
Remove from oil and drain on kitchen paper. Let them cool slightly for about 2 minutes.
Reheat the gochujang sauce until warm and fluid.
Toss the crispy chicken wings in the sauce until evenly coated and glossy.
Tip into a serving bowl and top with sesame seeds and sliced spring onions.

pro_tips: The cornflour (not regular flour) is the secret — it creates an ultra-thin, glass-like crust that stays crispy even after being tossed in sauce.
For extra heat, add a teaspoon of gochugaru (Korean chilli flakes) to the sauce. For a sweeter balance, add a splash of rice vinegar at the end.
